Rikako Ikee (front row, second from left) was a member of the Japanese women's 4x100m freestyle relay team that won silver at the Asian Games in 2023.

PHOTO: ST FILE

Published脗Mar 28, 2024, 05:47 PM

Updated脗Mar 28, 2024, 09:09 PM

TOKYO芒聙聯Leukaemia survivor Rikako Ikee will compete for Japan in the 100m butterfly at the Paris Olympics after missing out on an individual place at the Tokyo Games in 2021.

Three-time world champion Daiya Seto will swim the men芒聙聶s 200m individual medley after Japan芒聙聶s swimming federation announced its 27-member team for the Paris Games on March 27.

An 18-year-old Ikee was named Most Valuable Player of the 2018 Asian Games after claiming six golds and two silvers, and was expected to be one of the stars of the Tokyo Olympics.

In early 2019, a few months after those triumphs, she was diagnosed with leukaemia and spent around 10 months in hospital.

She returned to competition only in August 2020 and completed an incredible comeback by winning the 100m freestyle and 100m butterfly at the 2021 Olympic trials.

Her times were not fast enough to qualify for the individual events in Tokyo, but the performances gained her selection for both freestyle and medley relay teams at her home Olympics.

Now 23, Ikee secured her place in the 100m butterfly by just 0.01 of a second at Japan芒聙聶s trials earlier in March as she held off the fast-finishing Shiho Matsumoto to finish second in 57.30sec.

芒聙聹In the end, I think it was my long arms that won the touch,芒聙聺 Ikee was quoted as saying by Olympics.com.

芒聙聹I think God was on my side today.芒聙聺

Ikee had been training in Australia under coach Michael Bohl, where she has been focusing on increasing the number of dolphin kicks straight after the start of her races, which has been a weak point for her, according to The Japan Times.

She said before the trials: 芒聙聹I am confident I can beat the qualifying times. My aim is well beyond them.

芒聙聹I芒聙聶ve improved tremendously. I芒聙聶m approaching this meet with the attitude that I芒聙聶m absolutely going to Paris.芒聙聺

Her 100m fly race was won in an impressive 56.91sec by 17-year-old脗聽Mizuki Hirai, one of several talented Japanese teenagers in the team.

Also heading to Paris are Mio Narita, 17, in the women芒聙聶s 400m IM and Tomoyuki Matsushita, 18, who swims the same men芒聙聶s event.

芒聙聹We have a very balanced group of veterans and the young and those somewhere in the middle,芒聙聺 said Japan Swimming Federation president Daichi Suzuki, the 1988 Seoul 100m backstroke gold medallist.

芒聙聹I want to see them level up in the next four months and expect that they will show their will to fight in Paris. I am proud of the athletes we have here. I hope they will raise our flag in Paris.芒聙聺

Meanwhile, British Olympic diving champion Matty Lee, who won gold alongside Tom Daley in 2021, will miss the Paris Games following spinal surgery.

The British pair won the men芒聙聶s synchronised 10m platform at the Tokyo Olympics and Lee also triumphed alongside Noah Williams at the Commonwealth Games in 2022.

But the 26-year-old will miss the Olympics after receiving treatment.

芒聙聹The surgery went well, but my surgeon told me my nerve was very stuck and it took longer than expected to remove my bulging disc without damaging my nerve,芒聙聺 he said in an Instagram post.

芒聙聹He also told me it wouldn芒聙聶t have got better on its own, so to have the surgery was the right decision.芒聙聺

Lee added: 芒聙聹What this means for me this year is self-explanatory, which is very sad, but the reason I look so damn happy in hospital is that I芒聙聶m no longer in chronic pain. We芒聙聶ve found a solution and I have something to work on.芒聙聺

Daley, 29, still has a chance of winning a second straight Olympic gold after joining forces with Williams.AFP

title: Japanese leukaemia survivor Rikako Ikee finally qualifies for individual event at Paris Olympics | The Straits Times
description: She secured her place in the 100m butterfly by just 0.01sec at Japan芒聙聶s trials earlier in March. Read more at straitstimes.com.
